RTX_3090_Ti.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3090 Ti
RTX_3090_Ti.notes: Conservative clocks for stability. Can try up to 1950 MHz if cooling is adequate.

RTX_4090.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090
RTX_4090.notes: Conservative clocks. RTX 4090 can boost higher but may throttle under sustained load.

RTX_4080.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080
RTX_4080.notes: GDDR6X memory runs hot - conservative memory clock recommended.

RTX_4070_Ti.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4070 Ti
RTX_4070_Ti.notes: Good balance of performance and power efficiency.

RTX_3080.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3080
RTX_3080.notes: Conservative clocks for 10GB model. 12GB model may handle slightly higher.

RTX_3070.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070
RTX_3070.notes: Efficient GPU - recommended clocks provide good stability.

A100.name: NVIDIA A100-SXM4-40GB
A100.notes: Data center GPU - clocks are already stable. Locking mainly helps with consistency.

V100.name: Tesla V100-SXM2-16GB
V100.notes: Data center GPU - stable clocks by default.

RTX_4000_Ada_Mobile.name: NVIDIA RTX 4000 Ada Generation Laptop GPU
RTX_4000_Ada_Mobile.notes: Mobile workstation GPU - boost varies 60-175W. Conservative graphics clock for thermal stability.

RTX_2080.name: NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080
RTX_2080.notes: Turing architecture. Founders Edition boost is 1800 MHz, reference is 1710 MHz.

RTX_Pro_5000_Blackwell.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 5000 Blackwell
RTX_Pro_5000_Blackwell.notes: Professional workstation GPU with 48GB GDDR7. Conservative graphics clock for sustained workloads.

matching_rules.description: Rules for matching nvidia-smi GPU names to clock profiles
